title: Illinois passes first US law mandating AI safety audits - TCR 05/28/26
description:
Illinois lawmakers passed SB 315 by 110-0 in the House and 52-5 in the Senate mandating the first independent third-party safety audits of frontier AI developers in any US statute, the Chan Zuckerberg Biohub released its ESMC, ESMFold2, and 6.8-billion-protein ESM Atlas as open weights and open data, and Robinhood opened its brokerage platform and a virtual credit line to third-party AI agents while disclaiming responsibility for agent-generated decisions.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-28-2026/

0:00 Illinois SB 315 and the first binding third-party AI audit floor
1:54 A Statehouse Closes the Verification Gap
5:15 Robinhood opens brokerage and credit-card access to AI agents
8:26 Chan Zuckerberg Biohub's open-weights protein-biology stack
11:10 Nvidia $150B Taiwan spend and Huawei's Tau Scaling Law path
14:19 OpenAI Foundation's $250M Economic Futures commitment
18:16 Close

This episode covers Illinois SB 315 and the first binding third-party audit floor for frontier AI, Robinhood's Agentic Trading and Agentic Credit Card landing before FIDO authentication standards exist, the Chan Zuckerberg Biohub's open-weights protein-biology foundation stack, Nvidia's $150 billion annual Taiwan supplier spend against Huawei's 1.4nm Tau Scaling Law path, the OpenAI Foundation's $250 million Economic Futures commitment, and a Phase 2 trial of rogaratinib reaching SDH-deficient GIST cancer.

Illinois SB 315 closes the verification gap that Mira Murati's sworn testimony, the pulled federal pre-deployment testing executive order, and the UK AISI finding of GPT-5.5 matching Anthropic's restricted Mythos preview each separately exposed - California and New York established disclosure and risk-plan baselines earlier this year, and Illinois converts them into externally enforceable evidence. Robinhood's Agentic Trading and Agentic Credit Card connect through the Model Context Protocol layer the BadHost vulnerability disclosed earlier this week was found to compromise, with the FIDO authentication standards for delegated agent transactions still being drafted by Google, Mastercard, and the broader working group. The Chan Zuckerberg Biohub's coordinated release of ESMC, ESMFold2, and the ESM Atlas moves the frontier of protein design from a handful of well-resourced biotech platforms onto the open scientific commons, with wet-lab researchers reporting functional binders against novel targets in under 72 hours of inference time on consumer hardware. Nvidia's $150 billion annual Taiwan commitment and Huawei's HiSilicon Tau Scaling Law announcement land in the same news cycle, the 2022 US export-control regime being engineered around from both ends simultaneously. The OpenAI Foundation's $250 million commitment funds independent measurement infrastructure, worker transition support, and research into post-AI gains distribution arrangements not yet designed.
